texto: It is not uncommon for textbooks on language to have sections on the relationship 'between' language and society, as if these were two independent entities which just happen to come into contact occasionally. My view is that there is not an external relationship 'between' language and society, but an internal and dialectical relationship.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Norman Fairclough: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “It is not uncommon for textbooks on language to have sections on the relationship 'between' langu…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, discourse, language pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e language.
